html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part One

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 1 through 7

The Call of Yirmeyahu — The Covenant Lawsuit — The Temple Sermon — Peace Peace When There Is No Peace

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran — DSS shorter recension primary

DSS text aligns with Septuagint against the expanded Masoretic — approximately one eighth shorter

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 7 — THE CALL AND THE COVENANT INDICTMENT

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 1 The Call of Yirmeyahu — Before I Formed You in the Womb — The Almond Branch — The Boiling Pot

1 The words of Yirmeyahu the son of Khilkiyahu, one of the priests who were in Anatot in the land of Binyamin,

2 to whom the word of YHWH came in the days of Yoshiyahu the son of Amon, king of Yehudah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.

3 It came also in the days of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, and until the end of the eleventh year of Tzidkiyahu the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, until the captivity of Yerushalayim in the fifth month.

4 Now the word of YHWH came to me, saying,

5 Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you. I appointed you a prophet to the nations.

6 Then I said, Ah, YHWH my Creator — behold, I do not know how to speak, for I am only a youth.

7 But YHWH said to me, Do not say, I am only a youth, for to all to whom I send you, you shall go, and whatever I command you, you shall speak.

8 Do not be afraid of them, for I am with you to deliver you, declares YHWH.

9 Then YHWH put out his hand and touched my mouth. And YHWH said to me, Behold, I have put my words in your mouth.

10 See, I have set you this day over nations and over kingdoms, to pluck up and to break down, to destroy and to overthrow, to build and to plant.

11 And the word of YHWH came to me, saying, Yirmeyahu, what do you see? And I said, I see an almond branch.

12 Then YHWH said to me, You have seen well, for I am watching over my word to perform it.

13 The word of YHWH came to me a second time, saying, What do you see? And I said, I see a boiling pot, facing away from the north.

14 Then YHWH said to me, Out of the north disaster shall be let loose upon all the inhabitants of the land.

15 For behold, I am calling all the tribes of the kingdoms of the north, declares YHWH, and they shall come, and every one shall set his throne at the entrance of the gates of Yerushalayim, against all its walls all around and against all the cities of Yehudah.

16 And I will declare my judgments against them, for all their evil in forsaking me. They have made offerings to other gods and worshiped the works of their own hands.

17 But you, dress yourself for work — arise, and say to them everything that I command you. Do not be dismayed by them, lest I dismay you before them.

18 And I, behold, I make you this day a fortified city, an iron pillar, and bronze walls, against the whole land, against the kings of Yehudah, its officials, its priests, and the people of the land.

19 They will fight against you, but they shall not prevail against you, for I am with you, declares YHWH, to deliver you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a (4Q70) and 4QJer-b (4Q71) are the primary DSS witnesses for Yirmeyahu. The DSS text of Yirmeyahu represents a shorter recension — approximately one eighth shorter than the Masoretic — preserved also in the Septuagint. This shorter text is the earlier and more original form. Chapter 1 opens with Yirmeyahu's covenant call: the prophet known before formation in the womb, appointed to the nations. The DSS and Masoretic are in close agreement for Chapter 1.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Yirmeyahu in full. Verse 5 — before I formed you in the womb I knew you — and before you were born I consecrated you — I appointed you a prophet to the nations — is read as the covenant declaration of prophetic predestination: the covenant prophet's identity is established in YHWH's counsel before biological existence.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text preserves Chapter 1 with no significant variance from the DSS shorter recension. The call narrative — the almond branch vision and the boiling pot vision — is covenant prophecy in two registers: the almond as the symbol of YHWH watching over his word, and the boiling pot tilting from the north as the coming covenant judgment instrument.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — before I formed you in the womb I knew you. The covenant call of Yirmeyahu begins before biology. YHWH's knowledge of the prophet precedes his formation. The covenant appointment — I appointed you a prophet to the nations — is not Yirmeyahu's choice but YHWH's sovereign designation. Verse 6 — I am only a youth. The covenant pattern: the called one protests insufficiency. YHWH's response — verse 7-8 — is not the removal of the insufficiency but the covenant promise of YHWH's presence and words. The call does not produce competence — it produces commission backed by covenant presence.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 2 The Covenant Lawsuit — You Have Forsaken the Fountain of Living Waters — Broken Cisterns

1 The word of YHWH came to me, saying,

2 Go and proclaim in the hearing of Yerushalayim: Thus says YHWH, I remember the devotion of your youth, your love as a bride, how you followed me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.

3 Yisra'EL was holy to YHWH, the firstfruits of his harvest. All who ate of it incurred guilt — disaster came upon them, declares YHWH.

4 Hear the word of YHWH, O house of Yaakov, and all the clans of the house of Yisra'EL.

5 Thus says YHWH: What wrong did your fathers find in me that they went far from me, and went after worthlessness, and became worthless?

6 They did not say, Where is YHWH who brought us up from the land of Egypt, who led us in the wilderness, in a land of deserts and pits, in a land of drought and deep darkness, in a land that none passes through, where no man dwells?

7 And I brought you into a plentiful land to enjoy its fruits and its good things. But when you came in, you defiled my land and made my heritage an abomination.

8 The priests did not say, Where is YHWH? Those who handle the Torah did not know me. The shepherds transgressed against me. The prophets prophesied by Ba'al and went after things that do not profit.

9 Therefore I still contend with you, declares YHWH, and with your children's children I will contend.

10 For cross to the coasts of Kittim and see, or send to Kedar and examine with care. See if there has been such a thing.

11 Has a nation changed its gods, even though they are no gods? But my people have changed their glory for that which does not profit.

12 Be appalled, O heavens, at this — be shocked, be utterly desolate, declares YHWH.

13 For my people have committed two evils: they have forsaken me, the fountain of living waters, and hewed out cisterns for themselves, broken cisterns that can hold no water.

14 Is Yisra'EL a slave? Is he a homeborn servant? Why then has he become a prey?

15 The lions have roared against him. They have roared loudly. They have made his land a waste. His cities are in ruins, without inhabitant.

16 Moreover, the men of Nof and Takhpanes have shaved the crown of your head.

17 Have you not brought this upon yourself by forsaking YHWH your Creator, when he led you in the way?

18 And now what do you gain by going to Egypt to drink the waters of the Shihor? Or what do you gain by going to Assyria to drink the waters of the River?

19 Your evil will chastise you, and your apostasy will reprove you. Know and see that it is evil and bitter for you to forsake YHWH your Creator — the fear of me is not in you, declares YHWH of hosts.

20 For long ago I broke your yoke and burst your bonds. But you said, I will not serve. Yes, on every high hill and under every green tree you bowed down like a whore.

21 Yet I planted you a choice vine, wholly of pure seed. How then have you turned degenerate and become a wild vine?

22 Though you wash yourself with lye and use much soap, the stain of your guilt is still before me, declares YHWH.

23 How can you say, I am not unclean — I have not gone after the Ba'alim? Look at your way in the valley — know what you have done. A restless young camel running here and there,

24 A wild donkey used to the wilderness, in her heat sniffing the wind. Who can restrain her lust? None who seek her need weary themselves. In her month they will find her.

25 Keep your feet from going unshod and your throat from thirst. But you said, It is hopeless, for I have loved foreigners, and after them I will go.

26 As a thief is shamed when caught, so the house of Yisra'EL shall be shamed — they, their kings, their officials, their priests, and their prophets,

27 Who say to a tree, You are my father, and to a stone, You gave me birth. For they have turned their back to me, and not their face. But in the time of their trouble they say, Arise and save us.

28 But where are your gods that you made for yourself? Let them arise, if they can save you, in your time of trouble — for as many as your cities are your gods, O Yehudah.

29 Why do you contend with me? You have all transgressed against me, declares YHWH.

30 In vain I have struck your children — they took no correction. Your own sword devoured your prophets like a ravening lion.

31 And you, O generation, behold the word of YHWH. Have I been a wilderness to Yisra'EL, or a land of thick darkness? Why then do my people say, We are free — we will come no more to you?

32 Can a virgin forget her ornaments, or a bride her attire? Yet my people have forgotten me days without number.

33 How well you direct your course to seek love — so that even to wicked women you have taught your ways.

34 Also on your skirts is found the lifeblood of the guiltless poor — you did not find them breaking in. Yet in spite of all these things

35 You say, I am innocent — surely his anger has turned from me. Behold, I will bring you to judgment for saying, I have not sinned.

36 How much you go about, changing your way. You shall be put to shame by Egypt as you were put to shame by Assyria.

37 From it too you will come away with your hands on your head, for YHWH has rejected those in whom you trust, and you will not prosper by them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-a preserves significant portions of Chapter 2. The DSS text is in close agreement with the Masoretic for this chapter. The covenant lawsuit of Chapter 2 is the foundational legal indictment of the book: YHWH as plaintiff, Yisra'EL as defendant, the heavens as witnesses — verse 12. The two covenant violations of verse 13 — forsaking the fountain of living waters and hewing out broken cisterns — are the covenant diagnosis of the entire book.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 2 — I remember the devotion of your youth, your love as a bride, how you followed me in the wilderness — is read as the covenant recollection of the wilderness relationship: YHWH recalls the covenant honeymoon of the Exodus generation as the standard against which the present covenant failure is measured.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text preserves Chapter 2 without significant variant from the DSS. Verse 13 — they have forsaken me, the fountain of living waters, and hewed out cisterns for themselves, broken cisterns that can hold no water — is the covenant image that defines the entire prophetic diagnosis: substituting human-constructed religious systems for YHWH's living covenant supply.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — has a nation changed its gods, even though they are no gods? But my people have changed their glory for that which does not profit. The covenant accusation is comparative and devastating: even pagan nations maintain loyalty to their gods — however false those gods may be. Yisra'EL, the covenant people who actually know the living YHWH, exchanged him for what does not profit. The theological scandal is not that Yisra'EL worshiped — but that they exchanged the genuine covenant relationship for a counterfeit.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 3 Return O Faithless Yisra'EL — Faithless Sister Yehudah — Shepherds After My Own Heart — Acknowledge Your Guilt

1 If a man divorces his wife and she goes from him and becomes another man's wife, will he return to her? Would not that land be greatly polluted? You have played the whore with many lovers — and would you return to me? declares YHWH.

2 Lift up your eyes to the bare heights, and see. Where have you not been ravished? By the waysides you have sat waiting for them, like an Arab in the wilderness. You have polluted the land with your vile whoredom.

3 Therefore the showers have been withheld, and the spring rain has not come. Yet you have the forehead of a whore — you refuse to be ashamed.

4 Have you not just now called to me, My father, you are the friend of my youth?

5 Will he be angry forever — will he be indignant to the end? Behold, you have spoken, but you have done all the evil that you could.

6 YHWH said to me in the days of King Yoshiyahu: Have you seen what she did, that faithless one, Yisra'EL? How she went up on every high hill and under every green tree, and played the whore there?

7 And I thought, After she has done all this she will return to me, but she did not return, and her treacherous sister Yehudah saw it.

8 She saw that for all the adulteries of that faithless one, Yisra'EL, I had sent her away with a decree of divorce. Yet her treacherous sister Yehudah did not fear, but she too went and played the whore.

9 Because she took her whoredom lightly, she polluted the land, committing adultery with stone and tree.

10 Yet for all this her treacherous sister Yehudah did not return to me with her whole heart, but in pretense, declares YHWH.

11 And YHWH said to me, Faithless Yisra'EL has shown herself more righteous than treacherous Yehudah.

12 Go, and proclaim these words toward the north, and say: Return, faithless Yisra'EL, declares YHWH. I will not look on you in anger, for I am merciful, declares YHWH — I will not be angry forever.

13 Only acknowledge your guilt, that you rebelled against YHWH your Creator and scattered your ways to foreigners under every green tree, and that you have not obeyed my voice, declares YHWH.

14 Return, O faithless children, declares YHWH, for I am your master. I will take you, one from a city and two from a family, and I will bring you to Tziyon.

15 And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will feed you with knowledge and understanding.

16 And when you have multiplied and been fruitful in the land, in those days, declares YHWH, they shall no more say, The ark of the covenant of YHWH. It shall not come to mind or be remembered or missed — it shall not be made again.

17 At that time Yerushalayim shall be called the throne of YHWH, and all nations shall gather to it, to the presence of YHWH in Yerushalayim, and they shall no more stubbornly follow their own evil heart.

18 In those days the house of Yehudah shall join the house of Yisra'EL, and together they shall come from the land of the north to the land that I gave your fathers for a heritage.

19 I said, How I would set you among my sons, and give you a pleasant land, a heritage most beautiful of all nations. And I thought you would call me, My Father, and would not turn from following me.

20 Surely, as a treacherous wife leaves her husband, so have you been treacherous to me, O house of Yisra'EL, declares YHWH.

21 A voice on the bare heights is heard, the weeping and pleading of Yisra'EL's sons, because they have perverted their way and forgotten YHWH their Creator.

22 Return, O faithless sons — I will heal your faithlessness. Behold, we come to you, for you are YHWH our Creator.

23 Truly the hills are a delusion, the orgies on the mountains. Truly in YHWH our Creator is the salvation of Yisra'EL.

24 But from our youth the shameful thing has devoured all for which our fathers labored, their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.

25 Let us lie down in our shame, and let our dishonor cover us. For we have sinned against YHWH our Creator, we and our fathers, from our youth even to this day, and we have not obeyed the voice of YHWH our Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of Yirmeyahu aligns with the Septuagint in Chapter 3. Verses 6-11 — the comparison of faithless Yisra'EL and treacherous Yehudah — are preserved in the DSS. The covenant comparison is the theological device unique to Yirmeyahu: the northern kingdom's apostasy, though punished first, produced a more honest covenant failure than Yehudah's half-hearted pretense of return.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 15 — I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will feed you with knowledge and understanding — is read as the covenant promise of genuine covenant leadership to replace the faithless shepherds of Chapter 2:8 who transgressed against YHWH.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verse 12 — Return, faithless Yisra'EL — I will not look on you in anger, for I am merciful — is the covenant call to the northern kingdom that has been in exile since the Assyrian conquest. YHWH's covenant mercy reaches even to those most recently judged.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16 — they shall no more say, The ark of the covenant of YHWH. The covenant prophetic declaration that the physical ark — the most sacred covenant object in Yisra'EL's worship — will become irrelevant in the covenant restoration. The restored covenant community will not need the ark because YHWH's throne will be Yerushalayim itself — verse 17. The covenant presence that was localized in the ark will be universally accessible. The physical covenant symbol is superseded by the covenant reality it represented.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 4 Break Up Your Fallow Ground — The Foe From the North — The Earth Without Form and Void — The Daughter of Tziyon Gasping

1 If you return, O Yisra'EL, declares YHWH, to me you should return. If you remove your detestable things from my presence, and do not waver,

2 And if you swear, As YHWH lives, in truth, in justice, and in righteousness, then nations shall bless themselves in him, and in him shall they glory.

3 For thus says YHWH to the men of Yehudah and Yerushalayim: Break up your fallow ground, and sow not among thorns.

4 Circumcise yourselves to YHWH — remove the foreskin of your hearts, O men of Yehudah and inhabitants of Yerushalayim — lest my wrath go forth like fire, and burn with none to quench it, because of the evil of your deeds.

5 Declare in Yehudah and proclaim in Yerushalayim, and say: Blow the trumpet through the land — cry aloud and say, Assemble, and let us go into the fortified cities.

6 Raise a standard toward Tziyon — flee for safety, stay not, for I bring disaster from the north, and great destruction.

7 A lion has gone up from his thicket — a destroyer of nations has set out. He has gone out from his place to make your land a waste. Your cities will be ruins without inhabitant.

8 For this put on sackcloth, lament and wail, for the fierce anger of YHWH has not turned back from us.

9 In that day, declares YHWH, courage shall fail both king and officials. The priests shall be appalled and the prophets astounded.

10 Then I said, Ah, YHWH my Creator — surely you have utterly deceived this people and Yerushalayim, saying, It shall be well with you, whereas the sword has reached their very life.

11 At that time it will be said to this people and to Yerushalayim: A hot wind from the bare heights in the desert toward the daughter of my people — not to winnow or cleanse —

12 A wind too full for this comes for me. Now it is I who speak in judgment upon them.

13 Behold, he comes up like clouds — his chariots like the whirlwind. His horses are swifter than eagles. Woe to us, for we are ruined.

14 O Yerushalayim, wash your heart from evil, that you may be saved. How long shall your wicked thoughts lodge within you?

15 For a voice declares from Dan and proclaims trouble from Mount Efrayim.

16 Warn the nations that he is coming — announce to Yerushalayim: Besiegers come from a distant land — they shout against the cities of Yehudah.

17 Like keepers of a field are they against her all around, because she has rebelled against me, declares YHWH.

18 Your ways and your deeds have brought this upon you. This is your doom, and it is bitter — it has reached your very heart.

19 My anguish, my anguish — I writhe in pain. Oh the walls of my heart — my heart is beating wildly. I cannot keep silent, for I hear the sound of the trumpet, the alarm of war.

20 Crash follows hard on crash — the whole land is laid waste. Suddenly my tents are laid waste, my curtains in a moment.

21 How long must I see the standard and hear the sound of the trumpet?

22 For my people are foolish — they know me not. They are stupid children — they have no understanding. They are skilled in doing evil, but how to do good they know not.

23 I looked on the earth, and behold, it was without form and void — and to the heavens, and they had no light.

24 I looked on the mountains, and behold, they were quaking, and all the hills moved to and fro.

25 I looked, and behold, there was no man, and all the birds of the air had fled.

26 I looked, and behold, the fruitful land was a desert, and all its cities were laid in ruins before YHWH, before his fierce anger.

27 For thus says YHWH: The whole land shall be a desolation — yet I will not make a full end.

28 For this the earth shall mourn, and the heavens above be dark. For I have spoken — I have purposed — I have not relented, nor will I turn back.

29 At the sound of horseman and archer every city takes to flight. They enter thickets — they climb among rocks. Every city is forsaken, and no man dwells in them.

30 And you, O desolate one, what do you mean that you dress in scarlet, that you adorn yourself with ornaments of gold, that you enlarge your eyes with paint? In vain you beautify yourself. Your lovers despise you — they seek your life.

31 For I heard a cry as of a woman in labor, anguish as of one giving birth to her first child — the cry of the daughter of Tziyon gasping for breath, stretching out her hands: Woe is me — I am fainting before murderers.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of Yirmeyahu preserves Chapter 4 with the same content as the Masoretic. The vision of cosmic undoing in verses 23-26 — the earth without form and void, the mountains quaking, no man remaining, the fruitful land a desert — is the covenant anti-creation oracle: Yirmeyahu sees the covenant judgment in terms of the reversal of Bereshit 1. Creation is being unmade by covenant violation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 4 — circumcise yourselves to YHWH — remove the foreskin of your hearts — is read as the covenant demand for inner transformation over outward ritual. The physical covenant sign of circumcision points to the required covenant reality: a heart opened to YHWH's covenant claim.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 4. Verse 23 — I looked on the earth, and behold, it was without form and void — tohu va-vohu — is the deliberate reversal of Bereshit 1:2. The prophet sees the covenant judgment as the unmaking of creation. Where Bereshit 1 moves from tohu va-vohu to ordered creation, Yirmeyahu sees the covenant community's sin moving creation back toward tohu va-vohu.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19 — my anguish, my anguish — I writhe in pain. The prophet's intercession has a physical dimension: Yirmeyahu's body absorbs the covenant suffering he announces. The covenant prophet is not a detached announcer of divine judgment but the one through whom YHWH's grief over the covenant community passes. Verse 27 — yet I will not make a full end. Embedded within the vision of cosmic undoing is the covenant restraint: YHWH will not complete the destruction. The covenant remnant theology operates even within the most comprehensive judgment oracle.

THE COVENANT LAWSUIT AND THE FOE FROM THE NORTH — CHAPTERS 5 THROUGH 7

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 5 Find a Man Who Does Justice — A Nation From Afar — They Shall Not Make a Full End — Eyes That Do Not See

1 Run to and fro through the streets of Yerushalayim. Look and take note. Search her squares to see if you can find a man, one who does justice and seeks truth, that I may pardon her.

2 Though they say, As YHWH lives, yet they swear falsely.

3 O YHWH, do not your eyes look for truth? You have struck them down, but they felt no anguish. You have consumed them, but they refused to take correction. They have made their faces harder than rock — they have refused to repent.

4 Then I said, These are only the poor — they have no sense. For they do not know the way of YHWH, the judgment of their Creator.

5 I will go to the great and will speak to them, for they know the way of YHWH, the judgment of their Creator. But they all alike had broken the yoke — they had burst the bonds.

6 Therefore a lion from the forest shall strike them down — a wolf from the desert shall devastate them. A leopard is watching their cities — everyone who goes out of them shall be torn in pieces. Because their transgressions are many, their apostasies are great.

7 How can I pardon you? Your children have forsaken me and have sworn by those who are no gods. When I fed them to the full, they committed adultery and trooped to the houses of whores.

8 They were well-fed, lusty stallions, each neighing for his neighbor's wife.

9 Shall I not punish them for these things? declares YHWH. And shall I not avenge myself on a nation such as this?

10 Go up through her vine rows and destroy, but make not a full end. Strip away her branches, for they are not YHWH's.

11 For the house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah have been utterly treacherous to me, declares YHWH.

12 They have spoken falsely of YHWH and have said, He will do nothing — no disaster will come upon us, nor shall we see sword or famine.

13 The prophets will become wind — the word is not in them. Thus shall it be done to them.

14 Therefore thus says YHWH, the Creator of hosts: Because you have spoken this word, behold, I am making my words in your mouth a fire, and this people wood, and the fire shall consume them.

15 Behold, I am bringing against you a nation from afar, O house of Yisra'EL, declares YHWH. It is an enduring nation — it is an ancient nation — a nation whose language you do not know, nor can you understand what they say.

16 Their quiver is like an open tomb — they are all mighty warriors.

17 They shall eat up your harvest and your food. They shall eat up your sons and your daughters. They shall eat up your flocks and your herds. They shall eat up your vines and your fig trees. Your fortified cities in which you trust they shall beat down with the sword.

18 But even in those days, declares YHWH, I will not make a full end of you.

19 And when your people say, Why has YHWH our Creator done all these things to us? you shall say to them, As you have forsaken me and served foreign gods in your land, so you shall serve foreigners in a land that is not yours.

20 Declare this in the house of Yaakov — proclaim it in Yehudah:

21 Hear this, O foolish and senseless people, who have eyes, but see not, who have ears, but hear not.

22 Do you not fear me? declares YHWH. Do you not tremble before me? I placed the sand as the boundary for the sea, a perpetual barrier that it cannot pass. Though the waves toss, they cannot prevail — though they roar, they cannot pass over it.

23 But this people has a stubborn and rebellious heart — they have turned aside and gone away.

24 They do not say in their hearts, Let us fear YHWH our Creator, who gives the rain in its season, the autumn rain and the spring rain, and keeps for us the weeks appointed for the harvest.

25 Your iniquities have turned these away, and your sins have kept good from you.

26 For wicked men are found among my people — they lurk like fowlers lying in wait. They set a trap — they catch men.

27 Like a cage full of birds, their houses are full of deceit. Therefore they have become great and rich.

28 They have grown fat and sleek. They know no limits in deeds of evil — they judge not with justice the cause of the fatherless, to make it prosper, and they do not defend the rights of the needy.

29 Shall I not punish them for these things? declares YHWH. And shall I not avenge myself on a nation such as this?

30 An appalling and horrible thing has happened in the land:

31 The prophets prophesy falsely, and the priests rule at their direction. My people love to have it so, but what will you do when the end comes?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension is in close agreement with the Masoretic for Chapter 5. The covenant search of verse 1 — can you find one man who does justice and seeks truth? — is the covenant standard that determines the fate of the city. The parallel to Avraham's intercession for Sodom in Bereshit 18 is deliberate: the covenant city of Yerushalayim has fallen below Sodom's standard.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 22 — I placed the sand as the boundary for the sea, a perpetual barrier it cannot pass — is read as the covenant demonstration of YHWH's sovereign ordering of creation: the same Creator who restrains the sea with sand has set covenant boundaries for his people that they have transgressed.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement. Verse 31 — the prophets prophesy falsely, and the priests rule at their direction — my people love to have it so. The covenant diagnosis of institutional religious failure: prophet and priest collude, and the people prefer the false comfort to the covenant truth. The covenant failure is systemic and chosen.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — if you can find one man who does justice and seeks truth, I may pardon her. The covenant standard is the individual: one righteous person could tip the covenant scales for an entire city. This is the covenant logic of intercession. The city is searched at every social level — verse 4-5 — and found wanting at both the poor and the powerful level. The covenant indictment is total: no class of society maintains covenant justice.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 6 Flee From Yerushalayim — Peace Peace When There Is No Peace — Stand at the Crossroads — Rejected Silver

1 Flee for safety, O people of Binyamin, from the midst of Yerushalayim. Blow the trumpet in Tekoa, and raise a signal fire in Beit-Hakkerem, for disaster looms out of the north, and great destruction.

2 The lovely and delicately bred I will destroy, the daughter of Tziyon.

3 Shepherds with their flocks shall come against her. They shall pitch their tents around her. They shall pasture, each in his place.

4 Prepare war against her — arise, and let us attack at noon. Woe to us, for the day declines — for the shadows of evening lengthen.

5 Arise, and let us attack by night and destroy her palaces.

6 For thus says YHWH of hosts: Cut down her trees — cast up a siege mound against Yerushalayim. This is the city that must be punished — there is nothing but oppression within her.

7 As a well keeps its water fresh, so she keeps fresh her evil. Violence and destruction are heard within her — sickness and wounds are ever before me.

8 Be warned, O Yerushalayim, lest I turn from you in disgust, lest I make you a desolation, an uninhabited land.

9 Thus says YHWH of hosts: They shall glean thoroughly as a vine the remnant of Yisra'EL. Like a grape gatherer, pass your hand again over its branches.

10 To whom shall I speak and give warning, that they may hear? Behold, their ears are uncircumcised — they cannot listen. Behold, the word of YHWH is to them an object of scorn — they take no pleasure in it.

11 Therefore I am full of the wrath of YHWH — I am weary of holding it in. Pour it out upon the children in the street, and upon the gatherings of young men, also. Both husband and wife shall be taken, the elderly and the very aged.

12 Their houses shall be turned over to others, their fields and wives together, for I will stretch out my hand against the inhabitants of the land, declares YHWH.

13 For from the least to the greatest of them, everyone is greedy for unjust gain. And from prophet to priest, everyone deals falsely.

14 They have healed the wound of my people lightly, saying, Peace, peace, when there is no peace.

15 Were they ashamed when they committed abomination? No, they were not at all ashamed — they did not know how to blush. Therefore they shall fall among those who fall. At the time that I punish them, they shall be overthrown, says YHWH.

16 Thus says YHWH: Stand by the roads, and look, and ask for the ancient paths, where the good way is, and walk in it, and find rest for your souls. But they said, We will not walk in it.

17 I set watchmen over you, saying, Pay attention to the sound of the trumpet. But they said, We will not pay attention.

18 Therefore hear, O nations, and know, O congregation, what will happen to them.

19 Hear, O earth — behold, I am bringing disaster upon this people, the fruit of their devices, because they have not paid attention to my words, and as for my Torah, they have rejected it.

20 What use to me is frankincense that comes from Sheba, or sweet cane from a distant land? Your burnt offerings are not acceptable, nor your sacrifices pleasing to me.

21 Therefore thus says YHWH: Behold, I will lay before this people stumbling blocks against which they shall stumble — fathers and sons together, neighbor and friend shall perish.

22 Thus says YHWH: Behold, a people is coming from the north country, and a great nation is stirring from the farthest parts of the earth.

23 They lay hold on bow and javelin. They are cruel and have no mercy. The sound of them is like the roaring sea — they ride on horses, set in array as a man for battle, against you, O daughter of Tziyon.

24 We have heard the report of it — our hands fall helpless. Anguish has taken hold of us, pain as of a woman in labor.

25 Go not out into the field, nor walk on the road, for the enemy has a sword — terror is on every side.

26 O daughter of my people, put on sackcloth, and roll in ashes. Make mourning as for an only son — most bitter lamentation, for suddenly the destroyer will come upon us.

27 I have made you a tester of metals among my people, that you may know and test their ways.

28 They are all stubbornly rebellious, going about with slanders. They are bronze and iron — all of them act corruptly.

29 The bellows blow fiercely — the lead is consumed by the fire. In vain the refining goes on, for the wicked are not removed.

30 Rejected silver they are called, for YHWH has rejected them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 6 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 14 — they have healed the wound of my people lightly, saying, Peace, peace, when there is no peace — is the covenant diagnosis of false prophecy that defines the conflict between Yirmeyahu and the establishment prophets throughout the book.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 16 — stand by the roads, and look, and ask for the ancient paths, where the good way is, and walk in it, and find rest for your souls — is read as the covenant invitation to covenant archaeology: return to the covenant path established at Sinai rather than pursuing the innovations of covenant apostasy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 6. Verses 27-30 — the refinery metaphor — is the covenant image of testing that defines Yirmeyahu's prophetic function: he is the tester of metals, the assayer of the covenant community's quality. The result — rejected silver — is the covenant verdict on a generation that failed the covenant test.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — peace, peace, when there is no peace. The most concentrated covenant indictment in the entire book in six words. The false prophets who declare shalom shalom over a covenant community in active covenant violation are not merely wrong — they are covenant criminals. Their smooth words prevent the covenant community from recognizing its condition and making the covenant return that could avert disaster. The false peace is more dangerous than open hostility because it prevents the covenant response to danger.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 7 The Temple Sermon — Amend Your Ways — This Is the Temple of YHWH — The Valley of Slaughter

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH:

2 Stand in the gate of YHWH's house, and proclaim there this word, and say: Hear the word of YHWH, all you men of Yehudah who enter these gates to worship YHWH.

3 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Amend your ways and your deeds, and I will let you dwell in this place.

4 Do not trust in these deceptive words: This is the temple of YHWH, the temple of YHWH, the temple of YHWH.

5 For if you truly amend your ways and your deeds, if you truly execute justice one with another,

6 If you do not oppress the sojourner, the fatherless, or the widow, or shed innocent blood in this place, and if you do not go after other gods to your own harm,

7 Then I will let you dwell in this place, in the land that I gave of old to your fathers forever.

8 Behold, you trust in deceptive words to no avail.

9 Will you steal, murder, commit adultery, swear falsely, make offerings to Ba'al, and go after other gods that you have not known,

10 And then come and stand before me in this house, which is called by my name, and say, We are delivered — only to go on doing all these abominations?

11 Has this house, which is called by my name, become a den of robbers in your eyes? Behold, I myself have seen it, declares YHWH.

12 Go now to my place that was in Shiloh, where I made my name dwell at first, and see what I did to it because of the evil of my people Yisra'EL.

13 And now, because you have done all these things, declares YHWH, and when I spoke to you persistently you did not listen, and when I called you, you did not answer,

14 Therefore I will do to the house that is called by my name, and in which you trust, and to the place that I gave to you and to your fathers, as I did to Shiloh.

15 And I will cast you out of my sight, as I cast out all your kinsmen, all the offspring of Efrayim.

16 As for you, do not pray for this people, or lift up a cry or prayer for them, and do not intercede with me, for I will not hear you.

17 Do you not see what they are doing in the cities of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim?

18 The children gather wood, the fathers kindle fire, and the women knead dough, to make cakes for the queen of heaven. And they pour out drink offerings to other gods, to provoke me to anger.

19 Is it I whom they provoke? declares YHWH. Is it not themselves, to their own shame?

20 Therefore thus says YHWH my Creator: Behold, my anger and my wrath will be poured out on this place, upon man and beast, upon the trees of the field and the fruit of the ground. It will burn and not be quenched.

21 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Add your burnt offerings to your sacrifices, and eat the flesh.

22 For in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt, I did not speak to your fathers or command them concerning burnt offerings and sacrifices.

23 But this command I gave them: Obey my voice, and I will be your Creator, and you shall be my people. And walk in all the way that I command you, that it may be well with you.

24 But they did not obey or incline their ear, but walked in their own counsels and the stubbornness of their evil hearts, and went backward and not forward.

25 From the day that your fathers came out of the land of Egypt to this day, I have persistently sent all my servants the prophets to them, day after day.

26 Yet they did not listen to me or incline their ear, but stiffened their neck. They did worse than their fathers.

27 So you shall speak all these words to them, but they will not listen to you. You shall call to them, but they will not answer you.

28 And you shall say to them: This is the nation that did not obey the voice of YHWH their Creator, and did not accept discipline. Truth has perished — it is cut off from their lips.

29 Cut off your hair and cast it away — raise a lamentation on the bare heights, for YHWH has rejected and forsaken the generation of his wrath.

30 For the people of Yehudah have done evil in my sight, declares YHWH. They have set their detestable things in the house that is called by my name, to defile it.

31 And they have built the high places of Tofet, which is in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, to burn their sons and their daughters in the fire, which I did not command, nor did it come into my mind.

32 Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when it will no more be called Tofet, or the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, but the Valley of Slaughter — for they will bury in Tofet, because there is no room elsewhere.

33 And the dead bodies of this people will be food for the birds of the air, and for the beasts of the earth, and none will frighten them away.

34 And I will silence in the cities of Yehudah and in the streets of Yerushalayim the voice of mirth and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, for the land shall become a waste.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves the Temple Sermon of Chapter 7 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The Temple Sermon is one of the most politically dangerous declarations in the entire book — Yirmeyahu stands at the gate of the temple and announces its coming destruction. The parallel account in Chapter 26 records that this sermon nearly cost him his life.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 4 — do not trust in these deceptive words: this is the temple of YHWH, the temple of YHWH, the temple of YHWH — the triple repetition is the covenant formula being turned into a false security charm. The covenant community uses the covenant formula as a magic guarantee of protection regardless of covenant conduct.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 7. Verses 21-23 — add your burnt offerings to your sacrifices and eat the flesh — for I did not command burnt offerings when I brought them from Egypt — but this command I gave them: obey my voice — is the covenant declaration that the sacrificial system was never the covenant core. The covenant core was obedience. The sacrifices were covenant expression, not covenant substance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — has this house become a den of robbers in your eyes? The covenant indictment is precise: the people use the temple as a base of operations between acts of theft, murder, adultery, and false oath — as if YHWH's presence in the house protects the sins committed outside it. The temple becomes the robber's den not because robbery is committed inside it but because robbers shelter there. Verse 12 — go to Shiloh. The covenant historical precedent: YHWH abandoned Shiloh — the first covenant shrine — when the covenant community's sins reached the covenant threshold. The temple in Yerushalayim is not more protected than Shiloh was.

END OF PART ONE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 7
